message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: Measurement<->Interpretation fence and freeze() both bypassed by a duck-typed (non-abc.Mapping) mapping — yields a mutable 'frozen' Measurement that contains an Interpretation
DIMENSION: methodology integrity
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: src/assay_engine/methodology/fence.py 40-52 (_contains_interpretation) and 64-76 (Measurement.__post_init__); root cause in src/assay_engine/_frozen.py:61-102 (freeze)
DESCRIPTION: _contains_interpretation recurses only into collections.abc.Mapping and native list/tuple/set/frozenset; freeze() likewise deep-converts only those types. A duck-typed mapping (exposes items()/keys()/__iter__ but is NOT registered as collections.abc.Mapping — a common shape for lazy/proxy dict-likes in adapter code) is treated as an opaque leaf by both: the fence scan does not look inside it, and freeze() leaves it as the live, mutable object (it is hashable by identity, so freeze's final hash() check passes it through). Pass-1 issue #115 closed the Interpretation-as-dict-key vector for real mappings but missed the duck-mapping vector. CONCRETE PoC: class DuckMap with items/keys/__iter__ over {'k': interp}; Measurement(value=DuckMap(...)) constructs successfully (one-way fence breached — an Interpretation now sits inside a Measurement), m.value IS the original mutable DuckMap (not a FrozenDict), and m.value._d['k']='MUTATED' mutates a supposedly frozen Measurement payload after construction. So both the fence's one-way direction (METHODOLOGY §6) and the deep-immutability contract (_frozen module purpose) are defeated by the same input.
SUGGESTED FIX: Make both checks structural over the duck-typed mapping shape, not nominal: in freeze() and _contains_interpretation treat any object exposing keys()+__getitem__ (or items()) as a mapping (convert/recurse accordingly), or reject leaves that are mapping-like but not a recognized immutable type. Aligning freeze() and the fence scan on the same broadened mapping detection closes both the immutability and the one-way-fence bypass together.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this claim by examining the actual code.

content.content:
1	"""The measurement ↔ interpretation fence (METHODOLOGY.md §6).
2	
3	A hard boundary separates **measurement** (numbers the engine produces) from
4	**interpretation** (what they mean). Interpretation lives strictly downstream and cannot
5	feed back into measurement. Any use of judgment — including LLM-assisted classification — is
6	interpretation, and is labeled as such.
7	
8	These wrappers make the boundary explicit and type-visible in the pipeline. ``fence`` turns
9	a measurement into an interpretation; there is deliberately no inverse — you cannot turn an
10	interpretation back into a measurement.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from collections.abc import Mapping as AbcMapping
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Any, Generic, Mapping, TypeVar
18	
19	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze, freeze_mapping
20	
21	T = TypeVar("T")
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
25	class Interpretation(Generic[T]):
26	    """A judgment *about* one or more measurements. Downstream-only; never an input upstream."""
27	
28	    value: T
29	    basis: tuple[str, ...]  # refs (producer:inputs_hash) of the measurements this interprets
30	    rationale: str
31	    judged_by: str  # e.g. "operator" or a model identifier — judgment is always labeled
32	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
33	
34	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
35	        # Freeze the payload so the frozen record is deeply immutable + hashable (issue #29).
36	        object.__setattr__(self, "value", freeze(self.value))
37	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
38	
39	
40	def _contains_interpretation(obj: Any) -> bool:
41	    """True if ``obj`` is, or recursively contains, an :class:`Interpretation`."""
42	    if isinstance(obj, Interpretation):
43	        return True
44	    if isinstance(obj, AbcMapping):
45	        # scan KEYS as well as values — an Interpretation used as a dict key would otherwise be
46	        # smuggled into a Measurement undetected (#115)
47	        return any(
48	            _contains_interpretation(k) or _contains_interpretation(v) for k, v in obj.items()
49	        )
50	    if isinstance(obj, (list, tuple, set, frozenset)):
51	        return any(_contains_interpretation(v) for v in obj)
52	    return False
53	
54	
55	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
56	class Measurement(Generic[T]):
57	    """A value produced purely by the engine from data — no judgment involved."""
58	
59	    value: T
60	    produced_by: str
61	    inputs_hash: str
62	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
63	
64	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
65	        # Structurally close the one-way fence: an interpretation may never re-enter as a
66	        # measurement value — at the top level OR nested in a container/metadata (issues #22,
67	        # #30). Note: ref/inputs_hash integrity (forgery resistance) is delegated to the
68	        # provenance/audit layer, not enforced here.
69	        if _contains_interpretation(self.value) or _contains_interpretation(self.metadata):
70	            raise TypeError(
71	                "Measurement value/metadata must not contain an Interpretation — "
72	                "interpretation must not feed back into measurement (the fence)"
73	            )
74	        # Freeze the payload so the frozen record is deeply immutable + hashable (issue #29).
75	        object.__setattr__(self, "value", freeze(self.value))
76	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
77	
78	    @property
79	    def ref(self) -> str:
80	        """A stable id for this specific measurement: producer + input hash."""
81	        return f"{self.produced_by}:{self.inputs_hash}"
82	
83	
84	def fence(
85	    measurement: Measurement[Any], value: T, *, rationale: str, judged_by: str
86	) -> Interpretation[T]:
87	    """Cross the fence once, in the only permitted direction: measurement → interpretation.
88	
89	    ``basis`` records the specific measurement's ``ref`` (``producer:inputs_hash``), so an
90	    interpretation can be traced to the exact measurement it interprets — not merely to the
91	    producer (audit pass 1, issue #16).
92	    """
93	    return Interpretation(
94	        value=value,
95	        basis=(measurement.ref,),
96	        rationale=rationale,
97	        judged_by=judged_by,
98	        metadata={"inputs_hash": measurement.inputs_hash},
99	    )
100

1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
62	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
63	
64	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
65	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
66	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
67	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
68	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
69	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
70	    """
71	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
72	        return value
73	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
74	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
75	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
76	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
77	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
78	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
79	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
80	        return bytes(value)
81	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
82	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
83	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
84	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
85	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
86	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
87	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
88	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
89	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
90	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
91	        )
92	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
93	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
94	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
95	    try:
96	        hash(value)
97	    except TypeError as exc:
98	        raise TypeError(
99	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
100	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
101	        ) from exc
102	    return value
103	
104	
105	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
106	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`."""
107	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
108

content.text: The code matches the claim's description. Now let me write a PoC to verify the actual behavior.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cat > /tmp/poc_duck.py << 'EOF'
from assay_engine.methodology.fence import Measurement, Interpretation, _contains_interpretation
from assay_engine._frozen import freeze, FrozenDict

class DuckMap:
    """Exposes items()/keys()/__iter__/__getitem__ but NOT registered as abc.Mapping."""
    def __init__(self, d):
        self._d = dict(d)
    def items(self):
        return self._d.items()
    def keys(self):
        return self._d.keys()
    def __getitem__(self, k):
        return self._d[k]
    def __iter__(self):
        return iter(self._d)

import collections.abc as cabc
interp = Interpretation(value=1, basis=("p:h",), rationale="r", judged_by="op")

dm = DuckMap({'k': interp})
print("isinstance Mapping:", isinstance(dm, cabc.Mapping))
print("_contains_interpretation(dm):", _contains_interpretation(dm))

# Try to build a Measurement with an Interpretation hidden inside a duck map
try:
    m = Measurement(value=dm, produced_by="p", inputs_hash="h")
    print("Measurement CONSTRUCTED (fence breached)")
    print("m.value is dm:", m.value is dm)
    print("m.value type:", type(m.value).__name__)
    # Mutate the supposedly-frozen payload
    m.value._d['k'] = 'MUTATED'
    print("After mutation, m.value['k']:", m.value['k'])
    print("Interpretation still in m.value:", _contains_interpretation(m.value))
except TypeError as e:
    print("Measurement REJECTED:", e)

# Also test freeze() directly
f = freeze(dm)
print("freeze(dm) is dm:", f is dm)
print("freeze(dm) type:", type(f).__name__)
EOF
.venv/bin/python /tmp/poc_duck.py

content.content:
isinstance Mapping: False
_contains_interpretation(dm): False
Measurement CONSTRUCTED (fence breached)
m.value is dm: True
m.value type: DuckMap
After mutation, m.value['k']: MUTATED
Interpretation still in m.value: False
freeze(dm) is dm: True
freeze(dm) type: DuckMap

content.text: The PoC confirms the core claims. But note: `_contains_interpretation(m.value)` returns False *after* mutation because we replaced the interp with 'MUTATED'. Let me check whether the Interpretation actually survives inside the constructed Measurement before mutation, and whether DuckMap is even hashable (the claim says "hashable by identity").

hash(dm): 279276113
m.value['k'] is interp: True
type of m.value['k']: Interpretation
hash(m): -2337593474633848347
m.value has new key: True

content.text: Measurement is a public engine API (constructed by adapters/operators, not internally). The fence is meant to protect against exactly this kind of input. Let me check the §6 methodology doc and the freeze tests to confirm intent and whether the suggested fix is safe.

content.content:
83:- **Indeterminate** — the method cannot decide: underpowered, out of the measurement's
107:## 6. Method, not interpretation
109:There is a hard boundary between **measurement** (numbers produced by the engine) and
110:**interpretation** (what they mean). Interpretation lives strictly downstream and cannot
111:feed back into measurement. Any use of judgment — including LLM-assisted classification —
112:is treated as interpretation, fenced off, and labeled, so it cannot contaminate the
116:> not an automatic whole-pipeline retrofit. `assay_engine.methodology.fence` defines
117:> `Measurement` and `Interpretation`, and `Measurement` **refuses at construction** to hold an
118:> `Interpretation` (even nested) — so once a value is typed as a measurement, judgment cannot be
119:> smuggled into it. `fence()` crosses the boundary in the one legal direction
120:> (measurement → interpretation) and records the source measurement's ref. A study applies these
122:> wrapped as an `Interpretation` (`judged_by` = the model id), never passed where a `Measurement`

content.text: The documented contract is explicit: "Measurement **refuses at construction** to hold an Interpretation (even nested)". The PoC proves this contract is violated by a duck-typed mapping. Let me verify the suggested fix wouldn't conflict with the array-like handling (which also uses duck-typing via `keys`/`__getitem__` overlap), and check whether common real-world mapping-likes (e.g., `types.MappingProxyType`, which IS registered as Mapping) are the only concern.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cat > /tmp/check_fix.py << 'EOF'
import collections.abc as cabc
from types import MappingProxyType

# MappingProxyType - is it abc.Mapping? (already covered)
print("MappingProxyType is Mapping:", isinstance(MappingProxyType({}), cabc.Mapping))

# Would the suggested structural check (keys + __getitem__) wrongly catch array-likes?
import array
a = array.array('i', [1,2,3])
print("array has keys:", hasattr(a, 'keys'))
print("array has items:", hasattr(a, 'items'))

# str/bytes have neither keys nor items
print("str has keys/items:", hasattr("x","keys"), hasattr("x","items"))
EOF
.venv/bin/python /tmp/check_fix.py

content.content:
MappingProxyType is Mapping: True
array has keys: False
array has items: False
str has keys/items: False False

content.text:
All confirmed. The suggested structural fix (`keys()` + `__getitem__`, or `items()`) is sound and wouldn't false-positive on arrays or strings, and `MappingProxyType` is already covered by the existing `abc.Mapping` check.

Now let me assess severity realistically. This requires an adapter/operator to construct a `Measurement` over a non-standard duck-typed dict-like holding an `Interpretation`. Let me confirm this isn't blocked elsewhere and assess likelihood.

The findings are fully verified:

**Verified facts:**
1. `DuckMap` (items/keys/`__getitem__`/`__iter__`, not registered as `abc.Mapping`) → `isinstance(dm, abc.Mapping)` is `False`.
2. `_contains_interpretation(dm)` returns `False` even though it contains an `Interpretation` — the fence scan treats it as an opaque leaf.
3. `Measurement(value=dm, ...)` **constructs successfully** — the one-way fence (METHODOLOGY §6, line 117-118: "refuses at construction to hold an Interpretation, even nested") is breached.
4. `m.value is dm` is `True` and type is `DuckMap`, not `FrozenDict` — `freeze()` left it as the live mutable object (passes because identity-hashable).
5. `m.value['k'] is interp` is `True` before mutation — a real Interpretation sits inside a constructed Measurement.
6. `m.value._d['k'] = 'MUTATED'` and adding a new key both succeed after construction — deep-immutability contract broken.
7. `hash(m)` succeeds — the "frozen" Measurement passes downstream as if valid.

Both invariants (one-way fence + deep immutability) fail on the same input, and pass-1 #115 (closed Interpretation-as-key for *real* mappings) did miss this duck-mapping vector. The suggested structural fix is safe (arrays/strings lack `keys`/`items`; `MappingProxyType` already covered by `abc.Mapping`).

**Severity calibration:** This is a real integrity defect in a public engine API explicitly documented to be safe. However, it requires an adapter to (a) use a non-standard duck-typed mapping rather than dict/FrozenDict/MappingProxyType, and (b) place an Interpretation inside it — an atypical shape. It's a latent contract violation rather than a default-path break. I rate it MEDIUM (matching the assessor), not HIGH: the documented guarantee is genuinely defeated, but only via an uncommon input shape, and there is no evidence any in-tree producer constructs Measurements this way.

input.reasoning:
Independently confirmed via PoC against the real code (.venv python). The fence's one-way guarantee and the deep-immutability contract are both defeated by a single input: a duck-typed mapping that exposes items()/keys()/__getitem__/__iter__ but is NOT registered as collections.abc.Mapping.

Root cause matches the report exactly:
- fence.py:44 (_contains_interpretation) recurses only into isinstance(obj, AbcMapping) and native list/tuple/set/frozenset. A duck-mapping fails isinstance(AbcMapping) so it is treated as an opaque leaf and NOT scanned.
- _frozen.py:73 (freeze) deep-converts only isinstance(value, Mapping) and native sequences/sets; a duck-mapping falls through to the hash() check at line 96, which passes (objects are identity-hashable by default), so freeze() returns the live, mutable object unchanged.

Consequence: Measurement.__post_init__ (fence.py:69) calls _contains_interpretation -> False, so construction is allowed; then freeze() (line 75) leaves value as the original DuckMap. The result is a constructed Measurement that (a) contains a live Interpretation (m.value['k'] is the original interp), violating the documented contract in METHODOLOGY.md:117-118 ("Measurement refuses at construction to hold an Interpretation (even nested)"), and (b) is post-construction mutable (m.value._d['k']='MUTATED' and adding keys both succeed), violating the _frozen module's deep-immutability purpose. hash(m) still succeeds, so the corrupt record flows downstream looking valid.

This is a genuine production-readiness defect in a PUBLIC engine API (Measurement/fence are the documented integrity boundary that adapters/operators feed). Pass-1 #115 closed the Interpretation-as-dict-key vector for real abc.Mappings but did miss this duck-mapping vector, and the fence scan + freeze() share the same nominal (isinstance) blind spot.

Not HIGH: exploitation requires an adapter to use a non-standard duck-typed dict-like (not dict / FrozenDict / MappingProxyType, all already covered) AND to place an Interpretation inside it. That is an atypical shape; no in-tree producer constructs Measurements this way (Measurement is only defined in fence.py, constructed externally). So it is a latent contract violation on an uncommon input, not a default-path break — MEDIUM, matching the assessor's rating. The suggested structural fix (treat any object exposing keys()+__getitem__ or items() as a mapping in BOTH freeze() and _contains_interpretation) is safe: verified that array.array and str expose neither keys nor items (so array-like buffer handling at _frozen.py:85-94 is unaffected), and MappingProxyType is already an abc.Mapping.
input.evidence:
PoC output (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python):

PoC 1 — fence + freeze bypass:
  isinstance Mapping: False
  _contains_interpretation(dm): False
  Measurement CONSTRUCTED (fence breached)
  m.value is dm: True
  m.value type: DuckMap
  After mutation, m.value['k']: MUTATED
  freeze(dm) is dm: True
  freeze(dm) type: DuckMap

PoC 2 — interpretation actually present + record stays hashable + mutable:
  hash(dm): 279276113            (identity-hashable -> freeze passes it through)
  m.value['k'] is interp: True   (a live Interpretation sits inside the Measurement)
  type of m.value['k']: Interpretation
  hash(m): -2337593474633848347  (corrupt 'frozen' Measurement hashes fine -> flows downstream)
  m.value has new key: True      (post-construction mutation of a 'frozen' payload)

Fix-safety check:
  MappingProxyType is Mapping: True   (already covered)
  array has keys/items: False/False   (structural fix won't catch arrays)
  str has keys/items: False/False     (won't catch strings)

Code refs:
  src/assay_engine/methodology/fence.py:44-52  (_contains_interpretation — nominal isinstance only)
  src/assay_engine/methodology/fence.py:64-76  (Measurement.__post_init__)
  src/assay_engine/_frozen.py:71-102           (freeze — nominal isinstance Mapping, then identity-hash passthrough)
  docs/METHODOLOGY.md:117-118                   (documented guarantee that is violated)

DuckMap used: a class defining items()/keys()/__getitem__/__iter__ over a backing dict, NOT registered with collections.abc.Mapping (abc.Mapping.register or subclassing) — a realistic lazy/proxy dict-like shape.

content.text: Verified as a TRUE production-readiness defect at MEDIUM severity. Both the one-way fence and the deep-immutability contract are defeated by the same duck-typed-mapping input, confirmed by PoC against the real code.
